questions.hint: I + am + noun
questions.explanation: I के साथ हमेशा 'am' use होता है। 'a student' क्योंकि student consonant से शुरू होता है।

questions.hint: He + is + noun
questions.explanation: He/She/It के साथ 'is' आता है। Doctor consonant से शुरू होता है तो 'a doctor'।

questions.hint: They + are + noun
questions.explanation: They/We/You के साथ 'are' आता है। Friends plural noun है, कोई article नहीं।

questions.hint: I + am + adjective
questions.explanation: Be verb के बाद adjective आ सकता है। Happy = adjective।

questions.hint: She + is + adjective
questions.explanation: She के साथ 'is' आता है। Tired = adjective।

questions.hint: We + are + at + location
questions.explanation: Location के लिए at/in/on preposition use होता है। at home = घर पर।

questions.hint: This + is + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: This = यह, singular demonstrative। Is = be verb। my = possessive adjective।

questions.hint: You + are + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Very एक adverb है जो adjective को modify करता है। You के साथ are आता है।

questions.hint: The weather + is + adjective + today
questions.explanation: Weather के साथ 'the' article आता है। Is = singular subject के साथ।

questions.hint: I + am + an + noun (vowel से शुरू)
questions.explanation: Engineer 'e' vowel से शुरू होता है इसलिए 'an engineer'।
questions.grammarRule: I + am + an + [noun starting with vowel]

questions.hint: He + is + in/at + location
questions.explanation: 'in school' और 'at school' दोनों correct हैं। In = अंदर, At = उस जगह पर।

questions.hint: They + are + adjective
questions.explanation: They के साथ 'are' आता है। Tired = physical exhaustion adjective।

questions.hint: My mother + is + a + noun
questions.explanation: Mother = singular noun, so 'is'। A housewife = article + noun।

questions.hint: I + am + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Busy = adjective। Very = adverb that intensifies adjective।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective
questions.explanation: This = singular demonstrative। Water = uncountable noun। Cold = adjective।

questions.hint: We + are + all + noun
questions.explanation: All = emphasizer। Family = collective noun, no article needed here।

questions.hint: Today + is + day name
questions.explanation: Day of week के साथ कोई article नहीं आता। Today = subject।

questions.hint: My name + is + proper noun
questions.explanation: Name बताने के लिए 'My name is' सबसे formal तरीका है।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + color adjective
questions.explanation: Red = color adjective। Apple singular → is।

questions.hint: I + am + adjective (health)
questions.explanation: Fine = good health/condition. Common greeting response।

questions.hint: He + is + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Good = adjective before noun। 'a good person' = article + adjective + noun।

questions.hint: They + are + both + noun (plural)
questions.explanation: Both = दोनों। Brothers = plural noun। They are brothers।

questions.hint: I + am + in + city name
questions.explanation: Cities के साथ 'in' preposition use होती है।

questions.hint: She + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Beautiful = adjective describing appearance। Very intensifies।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective (size)
questions.explanation: Big = size adjective। Spacious more formal।

questions.hint: I + am + [number] + years old
questions.explanation: Age बताने के लिए 'I am + number + years old' pattern use होता है।

questions.hint: He + is + in/at + the + location
questions.explanation: Specific locations के साथ 'the' article आता है।

questions.hint: We + are + adjective
questions.explanation: Ready = adjective। We are ready = हम तैयार हैं।

questions.hint: This + is + my + noun
questions.explanation: This = यह, my = मेरा। House vs Home: house = building, home = where you live।

questions.hint: She + is + adjective (health)
questions.explanation: Sick/ill/unwell = health adjectives। Ill is more formal than sick।

questions.hint: I + am + a + profession
questions.explanation: Teacher consonant से शुरू → 'a teacher'।

questions.hint: They + are + adjective
questions.explanation: They = plural, so 'are'। Happy = emotion adjective।

questions.hint: This + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Expensive = price/cost adjective। Very = intensifier।

questions.hint: I + am + a + compound profession
questions.explanation: Software developer = compound noun as profession।

questions.hint: It + is + very + adjective + today
questions.explanation: Weather के लिए subject 'It' use होता है।

questions.hint: He + is + an + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Honest 'h' से शुरू होता है लेकिन vowel sound /ɒ/ है, इसलिए 'an honest'।
questions.grammarRule: Use 'an' before silent-h or vowel sound words

questions.hindi: मैं अपने काम में interested हूँ।
questions.english: I am interested in my work.
questions.hint: I + am + interested + in + noun
questions.explanation: Interested in = adjective + preposition combination।

questions.hint: She + is + angry + with + pronoun
questions.explanation: Angry with = adjective + preposition। With me = मेरे साथ।

questions.hindi: हम एक दूसरे के बारे में concerned हैं।
questions.english: We are concerned about each other.
questions.hint: We + are + concerned + about + each other
questions.explanation: Concerned about = adjective + preposition। Each other = reciprocal pronoun।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Complicated = complex adjective। Situation = noun।

questions.hindi: मेरा भाई एक IIT student है।
questions.english: My brother is an IIT student.
alternatives: My brother is a student at IIT.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + an + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: IIT starts with vowel sound /aɪ/ so 'an IIT student'।
questions.grammarRule: Use 'an' before abbreviations starting with vowel sound

questions.hint: They + are + very/highly + adjective
questions.explanation: Educated = past participle used as adjective। Highly = formal intensifier।

questions.hindi: मैं meeting के लिए available हूँ।
questions.english: I am available for the meeting.
questions.hint: I + am + available + for + the + noun
questions.explanation: Available for = adjective + preposition। 'the meeting' = specific meeting।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Important = high importance adjective। Crucial = more urgent।

questions.hindi: वह interview के लिए nervous है।
questions.english: He is nervous about the interview.
questions.hint: He + is + nervous + about + the + noun
questions.explanation: Nervous about = adjective + preposition। Interview = specific event।

questions.hindi: हम इस deal के बारे में confident हैं।
questions.english: We are confident about this deal.
questions.hint: We + are + confident + about + this + noun
questions.explanation: Confident about = adjective + preposition।

alternatives: This is a very attractive offer.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Attractive = appealing adjective। Quite = less intense than very।

questions.english: I am more experienced than you.
questions.hint: I + am + more + adjective + than + pronoun
questions.explanation: Comparative adjective: more + adjective + than। Experienced = 3+ syllables, use 'more'।

questions.hindi: यह रास्ता उस रास्ते से लंबा है।
questions.english: This road is longer than that road.
alternatives: This road is longer than that one.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective+er + than + that + noun
questions.explanation: Comparative: long → longer। Short adjectives add -er + than।

questions.hint: She + is + our + superlative + noun
questions.explanation: Best = superlative of good। Our = possessive adjective।

questions.hindi: मैं कल बहुत थका हुआ था।
questions.hint: I + was + very + adjective + yesterday
questions.explanation: Past tense में I के साथ 'was' आता है। Yesterday = past time marker।

questions.hint: He + was + at + location + yesterday
questions.explanation: Past tense में He/She/It के साथ 'was' आता है।

questions.hindi: हम पिछले हफ्ते Mumbai में थे।
questions.english: We were in Mumbai last week.
alternatives: We were at Mumbai last week.
questions.hint: We + were + in + city + last week
questions.explanation: Past tense में We/You/They के साथ 'were' आता है। Last week = past time।

questions.hint: They + were + all + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Were = past form of are। All = intensifier।

questions.hindi: मैं उस दिन office में था।
questions.english: I was in the office that day.
alternatives: I was at the office that day.
questions.hint: I + was + in + the + location + that day
questions.explanation: That day = उस दिन, past time reference।

questions.hindi: वह बचपन में बहुत शरारती था।
questions.english: He was very naughty in childhood.
alternatives: He was very mischievous as a child.
questions.hint: He + was + very + adjective + in + time period
questions.explanation: Was = past। Naughty/mischievous = childhood behavior adjective।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम कल school में थे?
questions.hint: Were + you + in + location + yesterday?
questions.explanation: Past tense question: Were + subject + in/at + place?

questions.hint: Was + he + adjective?
questions.explanation: Past question: Was + He/She/It + adjective?

questions.hindi: मैं उस time अकेला नहीं था।
questions.english: I was not alone at that time.
alternatives: I wasn't alone at that time.
questions.hint: I + was + not + adjective + at that time
questions.explanation: Was not = wasn't। Alone = adjective। At that time = time phrase।

questions.hindi: वे उस meeting में नहीं थे।
questions.english: They were not in that meeting.
questions.hint: They + were + not + in + that + noun
questions.explanation: Were not = weren't। That = वह (singular demonstrative for time/event)।

questions.hint: Are + you + adjective?
questions.explanation: Present question: Are + you + adjective?

questions.hint: Is + he + a + noun?
questions.explanation: Present question: Is + he/she/it + noun?

questions.hint: Is + this + adjective?
questions.explanation: This = यह। Correct/right/accurate = similar meaning adjectives।

questions.hint: I + am + not + adjective
questions.explanation: Negative: I am not + adjective। Unhappy = prefix 'un' से negative word।

questions.hint: He + is + not + adverb of place
questions.explanation: Is not = isn't। Here = यहाँ, adverb of place।

questions.hint: We + are + not + adjective
questions.explanation: Are not = aren't। We = plural, so are।

questions.hindi: मैं I'm + profession के रूप में contraction use करूँगा।
questions.hint: I'm = I am (contraction)
questions.explanation: I'm = I am का short form। Software engineer = compound profession।

questions.hindi: वह बहुत intelligent है — He's की contraction use करो।
questions.hint: He's = He is (contraction)
questions.explanation: He's = He is का short form। Intelligent = 4-syllable adjective।

questions.hindi: वे लोग office में नहीं हैं — aren't use करो।
alternatives: They are not in the office.
questions.hint: aren't = are not (contraction)
questions.explanation: Aren't = are not। Negative contractions informal writing में use होती हैं।

questions.hindi: वह meeting में था — wasn't use करते हुए नकारें।
alternatives: He was not in the meeting.
questions.hint: wasn't = was not (contraction)
questions.explanation: Wasn't = was not। Past negative contraction।

questions.hindi: वे school में नहीं थे — weren't use करो।
questions.hint: weren't = were not (contraction)
questions.explanation: Weren't = were not। Past negative plural।

questions.hint: She + is + an + abbreviation + noun
questions.explanation: MBA = /ɛm biː eɪ/ vowel sound से शुरू होता है, इसलिए 'an MBA'।
questions.grammarRule: Use 'an' before abbreviations starting with vowel sound

questions.hint: How + adjective + is + pronoun?
questions.explanation: How tall = height question। Is + he = be verb + subject (inverted)।

questions.hint: Where + are + you?
questions.explanation: Where = कहाँ। Are + you = be verb question।

questions.hint: Who + is + pronoun/noun?
questions.explanation: Who = कौन। Is + he = be verb question।

questions.hint: What + is + this/that?
questions.explanation: What = क्या। Is + this = be verb question।

questions.hint: What + is + the + noun?
questions.explanation: What's = What is। The problem = specific problem।

questions.hint: What + is + your + noun?
questions.explanation: Most common introductory question।

questions.hint: How + are + you?
questions.explanation: Standard greeting question।

questions.hint: When + was + he + adjective/adverb?
questions.explanation: When = कब। Was = past tense of is।

questions.hint: I + am + an + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Ambitious = vowel 'a' से शुरू होता है → 'an ambitious'।
questions.grammarRule: I + am + an + [adjective starting with vowel] + [noun]

questions.hint: Our + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Successful = achievement adjective। Highly = formal intensifier।

questions.hint: She + is + a + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Responsible = character adjective। Employee consonant → 'a'।

questions.hint: I + am + your + noun
questions.explanation: Your = तुम्हारा। Friend = relationship noun।

questions.hint: This + is + adjective + for + you
questions.explanation: Free = cost adjective। Complimentary = formal synonym।

questions.hint: They + are + quite + adjective
questions.explanation: Quite = थोड़ा less than very। Diligent = formal synonym of hardworking।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Famous = well-known। Popular = liked by many।

questions.hint: I + am + adjective (personality)
questions.explanation: Punctual = समय का पाबंद। Professional personality trait।

questions.hint: The + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: The child = specific child। Cute = appearance adjective।

questions.hint: Today + is + day name
questions.explanation: Day names = proper nouns, capital letter।

questions.hint: My + noun + is + time expression
questions.explanation: Birthday + is + time expression। Next month = future time।

questions.hint: They + are + near + the + noun
questions.explanation: Near = preposition of proximity। The hospital = specific building।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Popular = लोकप्रिय। Song = countable noun।

questions.hint: I + am + at + home + today
questions.explanation: At home = घर पर। Today = present time adverb।

questions.hint: She + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Creative = रचनात्मक। Adjective for talent।

questions.hint: We + are + from + country name
questions.explanation: From = से। Country name = proper noun।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Difficult/Hard/Tough = synonyms for challenge।

questions.english: My phone is on the table.
alternatives: My phone is on the table.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + on + the + noun
questions.explanation: On = surface के ऊपर। Table = flat surface।

questions.hindi: वह अपने काम में expert है।
questions.english: He is an expert in his work.
alternatives: He's an expert in his field.
questions.hint: He + is + an + noun + in + possessive + noun
questions.explanation: Expert = vowel 'e' → 'an expert'। In his work = prepositional phrase।

questions.hint: These + are + all + my + noun (plural)
questions.explanation: These = ये (plural demonstrative)। Are + plural noun।

questions.hindi: मैं इस काम के लिए qualified हूँ।
questions.english: I am qualified for this job.
questions.hint: I + am + qualified + for + this + noun
questions.explanation: Qualified for = adjective + preposition। Job/position = interview context।

questions.hindi: यह project deadline के लिए on track है।
questions.english: This project is on track for the deadline.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + on track + for + the + noun
questions.explanation: On track = idiom meaning progressing well।

questions.hindi: वे सब इस plan के लिए committed हैं।
questions.english: They are all committed to this plan.
alternatives: They're all committed to this plan.
questions.hint: They + are + all + committed + to + this + noun
questions.explanation: Committed to = adjective + preposition।

questions.hindi: मैं इस बारे में sure नहीं हूँ।
questions.english: I am not sure about this.
questions.hint: I + am + not + sure + about + this
questions.explanation: Not sure about = negative adjective + preposition।
questions.grammarRule: I + am not + sure/certain + about + this/that

questions.english: She is not available right now.
questions.hint: She + is + not + available + right now
questions.explanation: Right now = अभी। Not available = not free to talk/meet।
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is not + available/free + right now

questions.hindi: मैं Mumbai का रहने वाला हूँ।
questions.hint: I + am + from + city
questions.explanation: From = origin बताता है।

questions.hindi: वह एक बहुत dedicated teacher हैं।
questions.english: She is a very dedicated teacher.
questions.hint: She + is + a + very + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Dedicated = committed adjective। Very + adjective + noun order।

questions.hint: Our + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Strong = power/capability adjective। Capable = more formal।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Innovative = नया और उपयोगी। Professional adjective।

questions.english: He is admitted to the hospital.
alternatives: He is admitted in the hospital.
questions.hint: He + is + admitted + to/in + the + hospital
questions.explanation: Admitted to = preposition for entering institution। He is admitted।
questions.grammarRule: He/She + is + admitted + to + the + hospital

questions.hindi: हम एक दूसरे के लिए perfect हैं।
questions.english: We are perfect for each other.
questions.hint: We + are + perfect + for + each other
questions.explanation: Perfect for = adjective + preposition। Each other = reciprocal pronoun।
questions.grammarRule: We + are + perfect/made + for + each other

questions.hint: This + noun + is + not + adjective
questions.explanation: News = uncountable noun (always singular). True/false = truth adjectives।

questions.hindi: मैं अपनी job से satisfied हूँ।
questions.english: I am satisfied with my job.
alternatives: I am happy with my job.
questions.hint: I + am + satisfied + with + my + noun
questions.explanation: Satisfied with = content adjective + preposition।

questions.hint: She + is + an + abbreviation + noun
questions.explanation: MBA /ɛm/ = vowel sound → 'an MBA'।

questions.hint: My + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Strict = demanding/disciplined. Professional adjective।

questions.hindi: यह deal हमारे लिए beneficial है।
questions.english: This deal is beneficial for us.
alternatives: This deal is good for us.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective + for + us
questions.explanation: Beneficial = फायदेमंद। Formal business word।

questions.hindi: वे दोनों एक दूसरे के competitors हैं।
questions.english: They are both competitors of each other.
alternatives: They are both each other's competitors.
questions.hint: They + are + both + competitors + of + each other
questions.explanation: Competitors = plural noun। Of each other = possession phrase।
questions.grammarRule: They + are + both + [noun] + of + each other

questions.hindi: मैं इस project का in-charge हूँ।
questions.english: I am in charge of this project.
alternatives: I'm in charge of this project.
I am responsible for this project.
questions.hint: I + am + in charge of + this + noun
questions.explanation: In charge of = idiom for responsibility।

questions.hindi: यह situation मेरे control में नहीं है।
questions.english: This situation is not in my control.
alternatives: This situation is out of my control.
This situation is beyond my control.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + not + in + my + control
questions.explanation: Out of control = idiom। Beyond control = formal version।

questions.hint: She + is + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: Well-spoken = hyphenated compound adjective। Articulate = formal synonym।

questions.hindi: मैं इस company में नया हूँ।
questions.english: I am new to this company.
alternatives: I am a new joiner here.
questions.hint: I + am + new + to + this + noun
questions.explanation: New to = inexperienced in. Company = place of work।

questions.hint: He + is + very + adjective (personality)
questions.explanation: Patient = धैर्यवान। Don't confuse with 'patient' noun = hospital patient।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Important = महत्वपूर्ण। Crucial = more urgent/critical।

questions.hint: We + are + all + one + noun
questions.explanation: One team = unity expression। All = emphasizer।

questions.hint: He + is + a + compound adjective + noun
questions.explanation: First-generation = compound adjective (hyphenated)। Entrepreneur = business owner।

questions.hindi: पिछले साल मैं बहुत stressed था।
questions.english: I was very stressed last year.
alternatives: I was very stressed out last year.
questions.hint: I + was + very + adjective + last year
questions.explanation: Was = past tense। Last year = past time. Stressed = past participle as adjective।

questions.hindi: वह पहले एक छोटे शहर में था।
questions.english: He was in a small town before.
alternatives: He was in a small city previously.
questions.hint: He + was + in + a + adjective + noun + before
questions.explanation: Before = पहले। Small town = article + adjective + noun।

questions.hindi: जब मैं बच्चा था, मैं बहुत शैतान था।
questions.english: When I was a child, I was very naughty.
alternatives: When I was young, I was very naughty.
questions.hint: When + I + was + noun + comma + I + was + adjective
questions.explanation: Was = past, दोनों clauses में। Complex sentence with time clause।

questions.hindi: क्या तुम कभी America में थे?
alternatives: Have you ever been to America?
questions.hint: Were + you + ever + in + country?
questions.explanation: Ever = कभी। Were + you = past question।

questions.hindi: वे पहले colleagues थे, अब friends हैं।
questions.english: They were colleagues before, now they are friends.
alternatives: They used to be colleagues, now they are friends.
questions.hint: They + were + noun + before + now + they + are + noun
questions.explanation: Contrast: past (were) vs present (are)।
questions.grammarRule: They + were + [noun] + before, now + they + are + [noun]

questions.hindi: मेरे दादा एक freedom fighter थे।
questions.english: My grandfather was a freedom fighter.
alternatives: My grandfather was a freedom fighter.
questions.hint: My + grandfather + was + a + compound noun
questions.explanation: Grandfather = singular → was। Freedom fighter = compound noun।

alternatives: He used to be very shy.
questions.hint: He + was + very + adjective + before
questions.explanation: Before = पहले। Shy = introvert personality adjective।

questions.hindi: हम उस समय बहुत गरीब थे।
questions.english: We were very poor at that time.
alternatives: We were quite poor at that time.
questions.hint: We + were + very + adjective + at that time
questions.explanation: At that time = उस समय। Poor = financial adjective।
questions.grammarRule: We + were + very + [adjective] + at that time

questions.hindi: उस दिन मौसम बहुत ख़राब था।
questions.english: The weather was very bad that day.
alternatives: It was very bad weather that day.
questions.hint: The weather + was + very + adjective + that day
questions.explanation: Was = past। That day = specific past day।

questions.hint: The + noun + was + very + adjective

questions.hint: I + am + not + afraid + of + anything
questions.explanation: Not afraid of = negative adjective + preposition। Anything = कुछ भी।
questions.grammarRule: I + am not + afraid/scared + of + anything

questions.hindi: वह काम के लिए बिल्कुल unsuitable है।
questions.english: He is completely unsuitable for the job.
alternatives: He is not suitable for the job at all.
questions.hint: He + is + adverb + adjective + for + the + job
questions.explanation: Unsuitable = prefix 'un' + suitable। Completely = adverb।

alternatives: This problem can be easily solved.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Easily = adverb। Solvable = adjective (problem can be solved)।

questions.hint: I + am + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Financially = adverb modifying adjective। Independent = स्वतंत्र।

questions.hindi: वह नई job में बहुत comfortable है।
questions.english: She is very comfortable in her new job.
alternatives: She's very comfortable in her new job.
questions.hint: She + is + very + adjective + in + her + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Comfortable in = adjective + preposition। Her new job = possessive + adjective + noun।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Toxic = harmful adjective for relationship। Completely = adverb।

questions.hint: They + are + a + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Happy couple = adjective + noun compound phrase।

questions.hint: I + am + his + superlative + noun
questions.explanation: Best = superlative of good। His best friend = possessive + superlative + noun।

questions.hint: She + is + my + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Younger = comparative of young। Little = informal।

questions.hint: My + noun (plural) + are + adjective
questions.explanation: Parents = plural → are। Retired = past participle as adjective।

alternatives: We live in a joint family.
questions.hint: We + are + a + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Joint family = Indian culture compound noun।

questions.hindi: वह मेरे पिता की तरह है।
alternatives: He's like a father to me.
questions.hint: He + is + like + my + noun
questions.explanation: Like my father = comparison using 'like'।

questions.hint: My + plural noun + are + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Wise = बुद्धिमान। Grandparents = plural।

questions.hindi: वह एक very caring माँ है।
questions.english: She is a very caring mother.
questions.hint: She + is + a + very + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Caring = affectionate adjective। Mother = family noun।

questions.hindi: मेरा परिवार मेरी सबसे बड़ी strength है।
questions.english: My family is my greatest strength.
alternatives: My family is my biggest support.
questions.hint: My + noun + is + my + superlative + noun
questions.explanation: Greatest = superlative of great। Strength = noun।

questions.english: They are a very close-knit family.
questions.hint: They + are + a + very + compound adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Close-knit = hyphenated compound adjective for tight family bond।

questions.hint: My + noun + is + very + adjective (personality)
questions.explanation: Curious = जिज्ञासु। Inquisitive = more formal।

questions.hindi: वह मेरा सबसे प्यारा भाई है।
alternatives: He is my most beloved brother.
questions.hint: He + is + my + superlative + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Dearest = superlative of dear। Most beloved = alternate superlative।

questions.hindi: मैं depressed feel कर रहा हूँ।
questions.hint: I + am + feeling + adjective
questions.explanation: Am feeling = present continuous। Depressed = emotional adjective।

questions.hindi: वह बहुत anxious है exam के बारे में।
questions.english: She is very anxious about the exam.
alternatives: She's very anxious about the exam.
questions.hint: She + is + very + adjective + about + the + noun
questions.explanation: Anxious about = adjective + preposition। Anxiety = mental state।

questions.hint: I + am + very/highly + adjective
questions.explanation: Motivated = प्रेरित। Highly = formal intensifier।

questions.hindi: वह अपनी achievement से proud है।
questions.english: He is proud of his achievement.
questions.hint: He + is + proud + of + his + noun
questions.explanation: Proud of = adjective + preposition। Possessive + noun।

questions.hindi: मैं इस बात से बहुत disappointed हूँ।
questions.english: I am very disappointed with this.
questions.hint: I + am + very + adjective + with + this
questions.explanation: Disappointed with = adjective + preposition। With this = इस बारे में।
questions.grammarRule: I + am + very + disappointed/frustrated + with + this

questions.hindi: वह result से बहुत excited है।
questions.english: She is very excited about the result.
alternatives: She's very excited about the result.
questions.hint: She + is + very + adjective + about + the + noun
questions.explanation: Excited about = adjective + preposition।

questions.hindi: मैं उस घटना से shocked था।
questions.english: I was shocked by that incident.
alternatives: I was shocked at that incident.
questions.hint: I + was + shocked + by/at + that + noun
questions.explanation: Shocked by/at = past participle adjective + preposition। Past tense।

questions.hindi: वे सब परिणाम से खुश नहीं थे।
questions.english: They were not all happy with the result.
alternatives: They weren't all happy with the result.
questions.hint: They + were + not + all + adjective + with + the + result
questions.explanation: Not all happy = not every one was happy। With the result।

questions.hindi: वह उस decision से बहुत distressed था।
questions.english: He was very distressed by that decision.
alternatives: He was deeply upset by that decision.
questions.hint: He + was + very + adjective + by + that + noun
questions.explanation: Distressed by = adjective + preposition। Past tense।

questions.hindi: मैं जब छोटी थी, बहुत शर्मीली थी।
questions.english: When I was little, I was very shy.
alternatives: When I was young, I was very shy.
questions.hint: When + I + was + adjective + I + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Complex sentence with when-clause। Both was = past tense।

questions.hint: Are + you + adjective?
questions.explanation: Simple present question with be verb।

questions.hint: Where + is + pronoun?
questions.explanation: WH-question with be verb। Is + he = inverted subject-verb।

questions.hint: Are + they + adjective + right now?
questions.explanation: Right now = अभी। Free = available adjective।

questions.hint: Which + noun + is + pronoun?
questions.explanation: Which = कौन सा। Which person + is + he।

questions.hint: Which + noun + are + they + in?
questions.explanation: Preposition 'in' at end of question। Field = area of work।

questions.hint: Where + was + the + noun?
questions.explanation: Past question. Was = past be verb।

alternatives: Where were they a year ago?
questions.hint: Where + were + they + last year?
questions.explanation: Were + they = past question। Last year = past time।

questions.hindi: तुम उस समय कितने साल के थे?
questions.english: How old were you at that time?
alternatives: What was your age at that time?
questions.hint: How old + were + you + at that time?
questions.explanation: How old = age question। Were = past। At that time = उस समय।

questions.hindi: वह पहले कहाँ काम करता था?
questions.hint: Where + was + he + working + before?
questions.explanation: Was working = past continuous। Where = location question।

questions.hint: What + was + your + ordinal + noun?
questions.explanation: First job = पहली नौकरी। Was = past।

questions.hindi: मैं इस काम के लिए physically और mentally prepared हूँ।
questions.english: I am physically and mentally prepared for this work.
alternatives: I'm physically and mentally prepared for this task.
questions.hint: I + am + adverb + and + adverb + adjective + for + this + noun
questions.explanation: Two adverbs modifying one adjective। Physically and mentally।

questions.hint: She + is + a + compound-adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Results-driven = professional compound adjective।

questions.hint: This + noun + was + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Past tense। Unnecessary/pointless = negative evaluation adjectives।

questions.english: They are a culturally diverse team.
questions.hint: They + are + a + adverb + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Culturally = adverb। Diverse = adjective। Team = noun।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective (timing)
questions.explanation: Premature = too early। Decision timing adjective।

questions.hindi: मेरे team members बहुत cooperative हैं।
questions.english: My team members are very cooperative.
alternatives: My team members are highly cooperative.
questions.hint: My + plural noun + are + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Cooperative = मिलकर काम करने वाले। Team members plural।

questions.hint: This + is + a + compound noun/adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Landmark = ऐतिहासिक महत्व का। Formal/news context।

questions.hint: That + noun + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Unfortunate = दुर्भाग्यपूर्ण। Past tense।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: Heartbreaking = compound adjective। News = uncountable singular।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Informative = जानकारीपूर्ण। Media/reading adjective।

questions.hint: That + noun + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Insightful = गहरी समझ वाला। Past tense।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Comprehensive = व्यापक। Detailed = विस्तृत।

questions.hindi: मैं अपने career के बारे में optimistic हूँ।
questions.english: I am optimistic about my career.
questions.hint: I + am + adjective + about + my + noun
questions.explanation: Optimistic about = adjective + preposition।

questions.hint: She + is + a + adjective + compound noun
questions.explanation: Proactive = पहले से सोचकर काम करना। Problem-solver = compound noun।

questions.hint: Our + noun + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Productive = फलदायक। Past tense।

questions.hint: I + am + a + compound noun
questions.explanation: Team player = idiom/compound noun for cooperative person।

questions.hindi: वह बहुत analytical mind वाला है।
questions.english: He has a very analytical mind.
questions.hint: He + has/is + very + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Analytical = विश्लेषणात्मक। Mind = metaphorical noun।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: Time-sensitive = समय के प्रति संवेदनशील। Compound adjective।

questions.hindi: वे हमारे most valued partners हैं।
questions.english: They are our most valued partners.
questions.hint: They + are + our + most + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Most valued = superlative। Partners = plural।

questions.hindi: मैं किसी situation में panic नहीं होता।
questions.english: I am never panicked in any situation.
alternatives: I never panic in any situation.
questions.hint: I + am + never + adjective + in + any + situation
questions.explanation: Never = कभी नहीं। Panicked = adjective। In any situation।

questions.hindi: यह पूरा matter बहुत sensitive है।
questions.english: This whole matter is very sensitive.
alternatives: This entire matter is very sensitive.
questions.hint: This + adjective + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Whole/entire = adjective before noun। Sensitive = नाज़ुक।

questions.english: There is a communication gap between them.
alternatives: There's a communication gap between them.
questions.hint: There + is + a + compound noun + between + them
questions.explanation: There is = there's। Communication gap = compound noun।

questions.hindi: इस कमरे में बहुत अँधेरा है।
questions.english: There is a lot of darkness in this room.
questions.hint: There + is + a lot of + noun + in + this + room
questions.explanation: There is = existence pattern। A lot of + uncountable noun।
questions.grammarRule: There + is + a lot of + [uncountable noun] + in + [place]

questions.english: There is a lot of noise here.
questions.hint: There + is + a lot of + uncountable noun + here
questions.explanation: Noise = uncountable noun। A lot of = बहुत सारा।
questions.grammarRule: There + is + a lot of + [uncountable noun] + here

questions.hindi: इस office में बहुत staff members हैं।
questions.english: There are many staff members in this office.
alternatives: This office has many staff members.
questions.hint: There + are + many + plural noun + in + this + office
questions.explanation: There are = existence for plural। Many = quantifier।

questions.hint: He + is + very + adjective (personality)
questions.explanation: Reserved = कम बोलने वाला। Introverted = अंतर्मुखी।

questions.hint: My + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Outgoing = बहिर्मुखी। Personality = noun।

questions.hint: She + is + very + adjective (emotional quality)
questions.explanation: Empathetic = दूसरों की feelings समझने वाली।

questions.hindi: मैं एक very detail-oriented person हूँ।
questions.english: I am a very detail-oriented person.
questions.hint: I + am + a + very + compound-adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Detail-oriented = compound adjective। Professional trait।

questions.hint: She + is + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: Straightforward = सीधी बात करने वाली। Can be hyphenated or one word।

questions.hint: My + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Demanding = बहुत काम चाहने वाले। Boss = singular। My boss is।

questions.hindi: हमारी company culture बहुत inclusive है।
questions.english: Our company culture is very inclusive.
alternatives: Our workplace culture is highly inclusive.
questions.hint: Our + compound noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Company culture = compound noun। Inclusive = सभी को शामिल करने वाली।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: Cutting-edge = अत्याधुनिक। State-of-the-art = same meaning, more formal।

alternatives: That application is very easy to use.
questions.hint: That + noun + is + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: User-friendly = उपयोगकर्ता के लिए आसान। Compound adjective।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective (quality)
questions.explanation: Buggy = having many errors/bugs। Technical adjective।

questions.hindi: मेरा internet connection बहुत slow है।
questions.english: My internet connection is very slow.
questions.hint: My + compound noun + is + very + adjective (speed)
questions.explanation: Slow = धीमा। Internet connection = compound noun।

questions.hindi: वह website बहुत secure नहीं है।
questions.english: That website is not very secure.
questions.hint: That + noun + is + not very + adjective
questions.explanation: Not very = ज़्यादा नहीं। Secure = सुरक्षित।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective (technical state)
questions.explanation: Encrypted = past participle as technical adjective।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: AI = Artificial Intelligence। Intelligent = smart adjective।

questions.hint: My + noun + is + very + adjective (age)
questions.explanation: Old = पुराना। Outdated = more specific to technology।

questions.hint: The + noun + was + very + adjective (sport)
questions.explanation: Match = खेल। Exciting = रोमांचक। Past tense।

questions.hint: That + noun + was + very + adjective (entertainment value)
questions.explanation: Boring = उबाऊ। Dull = less strong। Past tense।

questions.hint: The + noun + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Amazing/incredible/fantastic = strong positive adjectives।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: Thought-provoking = सोचने पर मजबूर करने वाली।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + very + adjective (talent)
questions.explanation: Versatile = बहुमुखी प्रतिभाशाली।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Show = TV/web series। Popular = लोकप्रिय।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Addictive = नशे की तरह। Game = entertainment noun।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Creative = रचनात्मक। Director = profession।

questions.hindi: इस festival में बहुत colorful decorations हैं।
questions.english: There are very colorful decorations in this festival.
alternatives: The decorations at this festival are very colorful.
questions.hint: There + are + very + adjective + plural noun + in + this + noun
questions.explanation: There are = existence। Colorful = रंगीन। Decorations = plural।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Expensive = महँगा। Lavish = शानदार।

questions.hindi: मेरा interview कल बहुत अच्छा था।
questions.english: My interview yesterday was very good.
alternatives: My interview went very well yesterday.
questions.hint: My + noun + [time] + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Was = past। Very good = positive evaluation।

questions.hint: I + am + a + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Quick/fast learner = compound noun phrase। Common interview phrase।

questions.hindi: यह role मेरे लिए बिल्कुल perfect है।
questions.english: This role is perfect for me.
alternatives: This position is perfect for me.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + perfect + for + me
questions.explanation: Perfect for = बिल्कुल सही।

questions.hindi: मेरा CTC पिछले company में बहुत कम था।
questions.english: My CTC at the previous company was very low.
alternatives: My salary at my last job was very low.
questions.hint: My + noun + at + the + adjective + company + was + adjective
questions.explanation: CTC = Cost to Company। Was = past। Previous company।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + currently + adjective
questions.explanation: Vacant = खाली (for job). Currently = अभी।

questions.hindi: मैं पहले एक government employee था।
questions.english: I was a government employee before.
alternatives: I used to be a government employee.
questions.hint: I + was + a + compound noun + before
questions.explanation: Government employee = compound noun। Was = past। Before = पहले।

questions.hindi: वह छोटी company थी, लेकिन बहुत innovative थी।
questions.english: It was a small company, but it was very innovative.
alternatives: It was small but very innovative.
questions.hint: It + was + a + adjective + noun + but + it + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Contrast: small but innovative। Two adjectives connected by 'but'।
questions.grammarRule: It + was + a + [adj1] + [noun], but + it + was + very + [adj2]

questions.hindi: उस project का result बहुत disappointing था।
questions.english: The result of that project was very disappointing.
alternatives: That project's result was very disappointing.
questions.hint: The + noun + of + that + noun + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Disappointing = निराशाजनक। Past tense।

questions.hint: They + were + originally + a + noun
questions.explanation: Originally = मूल रूप से। Startup = new business noun।

questions.hint: This/That + noun + was + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Emotional/touching = feeling-evoking adjectives। Past tense।

questions.hindi: वह पहले एक very strict teacher थे।
questions.english: He was a very strict teacher before.
alternatives: He used to be a very strict teacher.
questions.hint: He + was + a + very + adjective + profession + before
questions.explanation: Very strict teacher = adverb + adjective + noun। Before = time।

questions.hindi: मैं तुमसे बात करने के लिए available हूँ।
questions.english: I am available to talk to you.
alternatives: I'm available to speak with you.
questions.hint: I + am + available + to + verb + to + you
questions.explanation: Available to + infinitive = ready/free for action।
questions.grammarRule: I + am + available + to + [verb] + to/with + you

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Practical = व्यावहारिक। Suggestion = advice।

questions.hindi: हमारे बीच का misunderstanding अब clear है।
questions.english: The misunderstanding between us is now clear.
questions.hint: The + noun + between + us + is + now + adjective
questions.explanation: Misunderstanding between us = compound phrase। Now = अब।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Valid = justified/legitimate। Completely = adverb।

questions.hindi: यह काम मेरे लिए नया है।

alternatives: This task is new to me.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adjective + for/to + pronoun
questions.explanation: New for/to me = unfamiliar। Both are correct।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + adjective (accuracy)
questions.explanation: Information = uncountable noun → 'is'। Wrong/incorrect = inaccuracy adjectives।

questions.hint: My + noun + has + changed / is + different + now
questions.explanation: Has changed = present perfect। Is different now = current state।
questions.grammarRule: My + [noun] + has changed / is + different + now

questions.hint: This + noun + is + very + adjective (complexity)
questions.explanation: Tricky = मुश्किल और पेचीदा। Informal word।

questions.hint: I + am + never + adjective
questions.explanation: Never fake = always genuine। Opposite: always genuine।

questions.hindi: यह परियोजना पर्यावरणीय दृष्टि से बहुत sustainable है।
questions.english: This project is environmentally very sustainable.
questions.hint: This + noun + is + adverb + very + adjective
questions.explanation: Environmentally = adverb। Sustainable = टिकाऊ/दीर्घकालिक।

questions.hindi: वह एक socially responsible company है।
questions.english: It is a socially responsible company.
alternatives: They are a socially responsible company.
questions.hint: It + is + a + adverb + adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Socially responsible = compound adjective phrase। CSR context।

questions.hindi: वह data publicly available नहीं है।
questions.english: That data is not publicly available.
questions.hint: That + noun + is + not + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Publicly = adverb। Available = adjective। Not publicly available।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Legally = adverb। Binding = adjective (must be followed)।

questions.hindi: मैं इस team का officially head नहीं हूँ।

alternatives: I'm not the official head of this team.

questions.explanation: Officially = adverb। Head = leadership noun।

questions.hint: That + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Outdated = पुरानी। Obsolete = और भी पुरानी।

questions.hint: This + noun + is + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Contractually = contract के अनुसार। Obligatory = अनिवार्य।

questions.hint: That + noun + was + adverb + adjective
questions.explanation: Globally = दुनिया भर में। Successful = adjective। Past tense।

questions.hindi: मेरे पिछले employer बहुत forward-thinking थे।
questions.english: My previous employer was very forward-thinking.
alternatives: My last employer was very progressive.
questions.hint: My + adjective + noun + was + very + compound adjective
questions.explanation: Forward-thinking = प्रगतिशील सोच वाला। Previous employer।

questions.hint: We + are + a + compound adjective + noun
questions.explanation: Cross-functional = different functions वाली team।

questions.hindi: यह plan कागज़ पर तो अच्छा है, लेकिन practically impossible है।
